name: upper_vaddr_no_progress
code:
proof fn phi_1_upper_vaddr_no_progress(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.upper_vaddr(layer, base) == base,
{
}
reason: If upper_vaddr equals the base, the layer maps a zero-sized address range, making page table traversal useless

name: entry_base_collapses_indices
code:
proof fn phi_2_entry_base_collapses_indices(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, idx1: nat, idx2: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        idx1 < arch.num_entries(layer),
        idx2 < arch.num_entries(layer),
        idx1 != idx2,
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, idx1) == arch.entry_base(layer, base, idx2),
{
}
reason: Distinct entry indices mapping to the same base address would mean page table entries alias, breaking address space partitioning

name: inv_allows_empty_arch
code:
proof fn phi_3_inv_allows_empty_arch(arch: Arch)
    requires
        arch.layers.len() == 0,
    ensures
        arch.inv(),
{
}
reason: An architecture with zero layers satisfies inv vacuously, yet cannot perform any translation—the invariant may be too weak

name: entry_size_non_decreasing_across_layers
code:
proof fn phi_4_entry_size_non_decreasing(arch: Arch, i: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        i + 1 < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_size(i) <= arch.entry_size((i + 1) as nat),
{
}
reason: Parent layers must map strictly larger regions than child layers; equal-or-smaller sizes would break the hierarchical decomposition of the address space

name: num_entries_allows_degenerate
code:
proof fn phi_5_num_entries_allows_degenerate(arch: Arch, layer: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.num_entries(layer) <= 1nat,
{
}
reason: A layer with at most one entry makes the directory level pointless and would collapse the page table hierarchy, indicating the invariant permits degenerate configurations
